question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the selectGenesis SQL query (which uses a CTE with a join on account_balance and record_file) on every call before genesis is cached, causing repeated expensive query execution under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the lack of a maximum response size limit in NetworkStatus(), where a very large address book causes peers.ToRosetta() to produce a massive JSON response that exhausts memory during serialization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each trigger nodeServiceEndpoint.toAddressBookEntry() to call strings.Split(n.Endpoints, ',') on a very long Endpoints string from the DB, causing excessive memory allocation per peer entry?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each construct a new rTypes.Allow struct embedding the full errors.Errors slice by reference, and if the serializer copies this slice, causing O(n*requests) memory allocation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each call currentBlock.GetTimestampMillis() which divides ConsensusStartNanos by int64(time.Millisecond), and if ConsensusStartNanos is 0 or negative due to a DB anomaly, cause unexpected behavior that propagates errors crashing the handler?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger GetRosettaBlockIdentifier() on both genesisBlock and currentBlock, each calling tools.SafeAddHexPrefix() on potentially large hash strings, causing string allocation overhead under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each iterate over the transactionTypes map via GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(), where map iteration order is non-deterministic and the resulting slice is appended to with OperationTypeFee, causing repeated allocations without any memoization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each independently call defer cancel() inside GetDbWithContext(ctx) for three separate DB operations (genesis, latest, entries), and if cancel() is not properly scoped, cause context leaks that accumulate and exhaust system resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the latestNodeServiceEndpoints query's string_agg function to produce an extremely long comma-separated endpoint string, which is then passed to strings.Split() causing large slice allocation per node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each call types.GetTransactionResults() returning the shared global transactionResults map, and if the map grows unboundedly due to a bug in init(), cause memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each trigger three separate defer cancel() calls inside the persistence layer, and if the underlying DB driver does not properly release connections on cancel, cause a connection leak that exhausts the pool?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() to scan into a []nodeServiceEndpoint slice with make([]nodeServiceEndpoint, 0), and if the DB returns a very large result set, cause unbounded memory allocation in the scan operation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each allocate a new rTypes.NetworkOptionsResponse with embedded rTypes.Allow, rTypes.Version, and full operationStatuses/operationTypes/errors slices, causing sustained heap growth that triggers frequent GC stop-the-world pauses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the selectLatestWithIndex query (ORDER BY index DESC LIMIT 1) on a record_file table without a proper index on the index column, causing sequential scans that saturate DB CPU under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the selectGenesis CTE query which joins account_balance and record_file tables, and if account_balance is large, cause repeated expensive full-table scans before the genesis block is cached via sync.Once?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each trigger fmt.Sprintf('%d', e.NodeId) inside ToRosetta() for every peer, and with a large address book, the cumulative string formatting overhead under high concurrency causes CPU saturation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger e.AccountId.String() inside ToRosetta() for every peer, and if the EntityId.String() method performs expensive formatting or allocation, cause CPU/memory exhaustion under concurrent load with a large address book?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each call persistence.IsTransactionResultSuccessful() for every transaction result code, and if this function performs a DB lookup or expensive computation per call, cause resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the latestNodeServiceEndpoints query with a file_id parameter that causes a full scan of the address_book table (no index on file_id), causing repeated expensive DB operations per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each trigger the full NetworkStatus() pipeline (3 DB queries + response construction) without any server-side concurrency limit, allowing a single client to spawn thousands of goroutines that exhaust available memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each build the operationStatuses slice by ranging over the transactionResults map, and since Go map iteration is randomized, cause cache-unfriendly memory access patterns that degrade CPU performance under load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the blockRepository.RetrieveLatest() to check rb.Index < br.genesisBlock.Index after a DB read, and if the genesis block index is uninitialized due to a race condition, cause a panic or incorrect error response that crashes the handler?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() to execute two sequential DB queries (for file IDs 101 and 102) when the address book is empty, doubling the per-request DB cost and amplifying resource exhaustion under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the latestNodeServiceEndpoints query's LEFT JOIN on address_book_service_endpoint, and if this table has no index on (consensus_timestamp, node_id), cause repeated expensive nested loop joins that saturate DB CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each allocate a new []*rTypes.OperationStatus slice with capacity len(types.GetTransactionResults()), and under high concurrency, the cumulative allocation of hundreds of slices per second causes GC pressure that degrades service availability?
